exact_reason: Strong post-hardening language is present, but social-post generation remains spam and harassment adjacent. Keep noindex until there is more evidence that responsible-use framing suppresses engagement-bait and platform-abuse intent.

Best next-batch candidate: relevance-first tag drafting, explicit YouTube/Google non-affiliation, no ranking views recommendation or engagement guarantees, complete FAQs/disclaimer, and clear output notes against keyword stuffing.

Hardening is materially stronger, but Instagram hashtag intent remains reach-chasing and trend-spam prone. Keep noindex until social hashtag pages prove they do not attract low-quality bulk-tag traffic.
No-viral framing and FAQs are strong, but TikTok hashtag demand is especially close to viral/reach manipulation and trend abuse. Keep noindex for now despite complete safety language.

Strong marketplace-safe candidate: truthful product-attribute framing, Etsy non-affiliation, no ranking sales traffic approval or search-volume guarantees, and useful FAQs for seller review before publishing.

Good future indexing candidate: pin/board/destination relevance is clear, Pinterest non-affiliation is explicit, traffic/ranking/saves/click guarantees are removed, and output notes discourage keyword stuffing.

Good niche candidate: genre mood instrument and context truthfulness are clear, SoundCloud non-affiliation is explicit, no plays discovery ranking or engagement guarantees are present, and FAQs/disclaimer are complete.

Rights-aware prompt hardening is strong, but named AI image platform intent still carries IP, likeness, unsafe image, and platform-policy risk. Keep noindex until AI-image prompt pages receive a separate indexing review.

Safety and rights warnings are complete, but AI image prompt intent remains sensitive because model outputs can involve IP, likeness, unsafe-content, and commercial-use ambiguity. Keep noindex for now.

FAQs and disclaimers are strong, but Helpful Content quality is still thinner than ideal for indexed acquisition because tool options and related links remain sparse and the brand-adjacent AI advice surface is broad. Add deeper prompt categories and adjacent workflow links before indexing.
